title: SignalDesk — First Customer Report

product.name: SignalDesk
product.summary: A lightweight workflow that turns scattered customer feedback into a prioritized evidence ledger.
product.outcome: Product teams spend less time consolidating feedback and can explain why an issue is prioritized.
product.pricing_motion: Founder-led B2B subscription with a design-partner pilot.

primary.user: Product managers at seed-to-Series A SaaS companies
primary.buyer: Head of Product or product-led founder
primary.job: Consolidate feedback from support, sales, interviews, and public discussions into defensible priorities
primary.trigger: Feedback volume grows beyond one person's ability to track manually
primary.current_alternative: Spreadsheets, shared documents, and manual tagging

adjacent.user: Customer-success leads at small B2B software companies
adjacent.job: Surface repeated customer problems and route them to product
adjacent.trigger: Expansion or churn reviews reveal inconsistent feedback follow-through
adjacent.current_alternative: CRM notes, support tags, and recurring internal meetings

disqualifiers: Teams with fewer than ten meaningful feedback items per month
Enterprises requiring an immediate on-premise deployment
Prospects without a visible product-feedback workflow or trigger

research.scope: Synthetic fixture using public-style product discussions and official pages from the prior 90 days

verdict.summary: The strongest initial wedge is small SaaS product teams visibly struggling to reconcile feedback across tools before roadmap reviews.
rationale: All shortlisted prospects show a public pain or timing signal tied to feedback consolidation.
The highest-confidence prospect explicitly describes the manual workaround.
Buying authority and current urgency still require confirmation through manual conversations.

prospects.name: Northstar Labs
prospects.pain_signal: A product lead publicly described copying feedback from support and sales into a spreadsheet before every roadmap review.
prospects.why_fit: SignalDesk replaces the evidenced consolidation step while preserving a source trail for prioritization decisions.
prospects.why_now: The discussion was posted this month and describes an active recurring workflow.

observed: The public post describes feedback arriving through support and sales.
The team manually copies the feedback into a spreadsheet before roadmap review.
inferred: The repeated pre-review work may be costly enough to justify a design-partner pilot.

sources.title: How are small product teams consolidating feedback?
sources.evidence: The author outlines a recurring copy-and-tag spreadsheet workflow and asks how peers avoid losing context.

contact.rationale: The original public discussion is the most contextual and least presumptive route.

outreach.offer: A two-minute example of an evidence-led feedback ledger built from the workflow described.
outreach.cta: Ask whether the author wants the example or can route it to whoever owns roadmap preparation.
outreach.opener: Saw your description of copying support and sales feedback into a roadmap spreadsheet. We are testing a source-linked feedback ledger for that exact review workflow. Worth sharing a two-minute example, or is someone else on the product team closer to this process?
outreach.likely_objection: The existing spreadsheet may feel adequate despite its manual cost.

prospects.caution: Confirm that the workflow remains active and do not imply that a public question signals purchase intent.

prospects.name: OrbitStack
prospects.pain_signal: A public issue requests a way to deduplicate related feature requests while retaining links to the original discussions.
prospects.why_fit: The requested deduplication and provenance closely match SignalDesk's core outcome.
prospects.why_now: The issue is open and has recent maintainer activity.

observed: The issue asks for deduplication of related requests.
The issue explicitly requires links back to original discussions.
inferred: Maintainer activity suggests the workflow is still under active consideration.

sources.title: Preserve request provenance when merging duplicates
sources.evidence: The issue asks for a repeatable way to group feedback without losing the source trail.

contact.rationale: The public issue permits a contextual question, but maintainership does not prove buying authority.

outreach.offer: A small mockup showing grouped requests with retained source links.
outreach.cta: Ask whether the mockup reflects the requirement before discussing any product.
outreach.opener: Your issue about merging related requests without losing their source links maps closely to a workflow we are prototyping. I can share a small mockup of grouped feedback with retained provenance. Would that help validate the requirement, or should I leave the discussion focused on the current implementation?
outreach.likely_objection: The team may prefer a native open-source implementation rather than a separate product.

prospects.caution: Treat the repository as a technical signal, not proof of budget or commercial interest.

prospects.name: HarborCloud
prospects.pain_signal: An official product-operations job post says the new hire will centralize customer feedback from several go-to-market teams.
prospects.why_fit: The planned responsibility implies a cross-team feedback workflow that SignalDesk is designed to support.
prospects.why_now: The company is actively creating ownership for the workflow.

observed: The official job post assigns responsibility for centralizing customer feedback.
The role works across support, success, sales, and product.
inferred: The company may be evaluating process or tooling changes as the role is established.

sources.title: Product Operations Manager
sources.evidence: The responsibilities include creating one feedback intake and prioritization process across four teams.

contact.target_role: Head of Product or incoming Product Operations Manager
contact.rationale: Only an official company route is visible; no direct relevant public profile was found.

outreach.offer: A concise checklist for setting up an auditable cross-team feedback intake.
outreach.cta: Ask whether the checklist should be routed to product leadership or the incoming operations owner.
outreach.opener: I noticed the Product Operations role will centralize feedback across support, success, sales, and product. We have a short setup checklist for keeping that intake source-linked and reviewable. Worth routing it to product leadership now, or is it better saved for the incoming operations owner?
outreach.likely_objection: The company may be defining the process before selecting any tooling.

prospects.caution: A hiring trigger establishes timing, not a confirmed tooling problem.

patterns.title: Feedback loses context during consolidation
patterns.insight: The strongest product message is not generic collection; it is retaining provenance while grouping repeated feedback.

patterns.title: Cross-team intake creates ownership pressure
patterns.insight: Roadmap reviews and new product-operations ownership are practical adoption triggers.

outreach_plan.angle: Validate the provenance problem before offering software.

steps.action: Open every source again, confirm the signal remains public, and contact only the two highest-scoring prospects through the documented route.
steps.success_signal: One reply that confirms or corrects the workflow.

steps.action: Send the promised mockup or checklist only to prospects who accepted the first CTA.
steps.success_signal: A prospect identifies a missing requirement or asks to involve the workflow owner.

steps.action: Follow up once with new evidence from the conversation, then close the loop without repeated messaging.
steps.success_signal: One design-partner conversation is scheduled or the hypothesis is explicitly rejected.

outreach_plan.success_metric: Three substantive conversations and one design-partner commitment within seven days.

limitations: This is a synthetic test fixture; its example.com sources do not represent real prospects.
Public signals establish relevance, not consent, budget, authority, or intent to buy.
Current workflow, ownership, and urgency must be confirmed manually before any pitch.
